---
title: GLASS
alias: &ALIAS glass
href: https://www.who.int/initiatives/glass
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Global Antimicrobial Resistance and Use Surveillance System (GLASS) is a WHO initiative that standardizes global AMR surveillance to understand the magnitude and trends of AMR and guide interventions.

## Key Features

- Collects standardized AMR and antimicrobial use (AMU) data from WHO member states globally
- Covers priority pathogens and infection sites per WHO AMR action plan targets
- Provides country-level data on resistance rates, sample sources, and patient demographics
- Publishes annual GLASS reports with global AMR burden estimates and data quality assessments
- Integrates with WHONET software for laboratory-level data collection and submission

## Use Cases in Research

- Accessing globally harmonized AMR prevalence data for low- and middle-income country contexts
- Comparing AMR rates and antimicrobial use patterns across WHO regions
- Informing global health models of AMR burden and the impact of stewardship interventions
- Contextualizing local AMR findings within the global WHO GLASS framework
- Supporting One Health AMR research by linking human, animal, and environmental surveillance contexts

## Getting Started

Visit [GLASS](https://www.who.int/initiatives/glass) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
